PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Continued To Nov-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Toriano in Propria Persona.

The Court acknowledged that this is for an administrator in pro per, that the court is reluctant, that probating a will is procedurally complicated with many steps and that you need to know what those legal steps are. Mr. Toriano stated that he is following Nolo, that this is a simple estate, that the assets consist of an IRA and general account at Schwab, a Fidelity account, that there is no vehicle and no real estate, that the liabilities are minimal and recited what he believed the next steps to be. The Court stated that Mr. Toriano would have to prepare an Inventory & Appraisal, would need a referee to appraise the securities and asked about bond. Mr. Toriano stated that he already has the bond in the amount of $10,000.00. The Court stated that it would have to be in the full amount of the estate for $650,000.00. Todd Christensen (bonding agent) stepped forward and stated that per 14.39 they had issued a bond in the amount of $10,000.00. The Court stated that it is not waiving bond with an out of state administrator who is in Pro Per and that bond will be required for the full amount of the estate. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order) with a status date of April 12, 2017 for status on close of estate, that the inventory and appraisal will be due promptly and that a probate referee will be appointed.

Subsequently the petitioner asked that the matter be recalled. Attorney Mary Mayo stated that Mr. Toriano is unable to obtain the necessary bond, that she will be substituting in and asked that the order not be signed until that time. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to November 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
